---
pagetitle: "Enrique Escalante"
---

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="27%"}

![](images/profile.svg){.profile-photo}

::: {.home-links}
[eescalante\@angelo.edu](mailto:eescalante@angelo.edu)
[Curriculum Vitae](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1e-_bLVNg0Z8_s7XfpIwmXEeJi2q2iyGb/view?usp=drive_link)
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=W0YLItcAAAAJ&hl=en)
[SSRN](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/cf_dev/AbsByAuth.cfm?per_id=2602348)
:::

:::

::: {.column width="3%"}
:::

::: {.column width="70%"}

# Enrique Escalante

**Assistant Professor of Economics**
Norris-Vincent College of Business, Angelo State University

**Research Assistant Professor**
Free Market Institute, Texas Tech University

---

Welcome to my website. I am an Assistant Professor of Economics at the Norris-Vincent College of Business at Angelo State University and a Research Assistant Professor at the Free Market Institute at Texas Tech University.

My research lies at the intersection of political economy and institutional economics. I study how informal governance, political violence, and state capacity shape economic and social outcomes, with a focus on Latin America. A second research stream examines the long-run effects of welfare state expansion on family structure in the United States.

:::

::::
